# Pull Request

## What does this PR do?

<!-- One or two sentences describing the change. -->

## Why?

<!-- Link to the issue this PR addresses. If there's no issue, explain the motivation. -->

Closes #

## Type of change

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] 📝 Documentation only
- [ ] 🧹 Refactor (no behavior change)
- [ ] 🧪 Test addition or fix
- [ ] ⚙️ CI / build / tooling

## How has this been tested?

<!-- Describe the tests you ran. Paste minimal reproduction code if helpful. -->

- [ ] `pytest -q` passes locally
- [ ] Added/updated tests for the changed behavior
- [ ] Manually verified with a real LLM provider (specify which):

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's [code style](../CONTRIBUTING.md#code-style)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (`pre-commit run --all-files`)
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix / feature works
- [ ] New and existing tests pass locally
- [ ] I have updated `docs/` if public API changed
- [ ] I have updated `CHANGELOG.md` under the `[Unreleased]` section
- [ ] No secrets, tokens, API keys, or `.env` contents in the diff
- [ ] Commit messages follow [conventional commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)

## Screenshots / recordings

<!-- For UI or notebook changes, attach a screenshot. For streaming/event changes, a recording or sample event log helps. -->
